registry.description: Foolishness is not mere intellectual error but the moral condition of the person who acts as if God does not exist — who operates from a fundamentally disordered understanding of reality. The Hebrew wisdom vocabulary is clear: the fool is not unintelligent but is morally and spiritually disordered, unable to see what is truly there. The Greek adds the note of empty talk and the proud disregard for what is genuinely wise. Foolishness has a direction: it moves away from God, away from reality, toward what merely seems attractive.

terms.lsj_entry:
ἀφροσύνη, ἡ, 
(ἄφρων) folly, thoughtlessness, frequently in plural, παῖδας καταπαυέμεν ἀφροσυνάων[Refs 8th c.BC+]; opposed to σωφροσύνη and σοφία, [Refs 5th c.BC+]

terms.medium_def:
foolish, ignorant 
unwise, inconsiderate, simple, foolish, Lk. 11:40; 12:20; 1Cor. 15:36; ignorant, religiously unenlightened, ignorant Rom. 2:20; Eph. 5:17; 1Pet. 2:15; boastfully foolish, vain, 2Cor. 11:16, 19
terms.lsj_entry:
ἄφρων, ον, 
genitive ονος, (φρήν) senseless, of statues, [Refs 5th c.BC+]:— and so, crazed, frantic, ἄφρονα κούρην[Refs 8th c.BC+]; silly, foolish, [Refs 8th c.BC+]; τὸ ἄ., ={ἀφροσύνη}, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; τῶ φρονίμῳ τῆς γνώμης παύοντες τὸ ἄ. [τῆς ῥώμης] [Refs 5th c.BC+]: comparative -έστερος[Refs 5th c.BC+]: superlative -έστατος[Refs 5th c.BC+]. adverb -νως senselessly, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; opposed to νοῦν ἐχόντως, [Refs 5th c.BC+]: comparative -εστέρως[Refs 5th c.BC+] -έστερον [Refs 4th c.AD+]
__2 ἄφρων, ={κώνειον}, Ps.-[Refs 1st c.AD+]

terms.medium_def: primarily dull; foolish, Mt. 7:26; 23:17; 25:2f., 8; 1Cor. 1:25, 27; 3:18; 4:10; 2Tim. 2:23; Tit. 3:9; from the Hebrew, a fool in senseless wickedness, Mt. 5:22*
terms.lsj_entry:
μωρός, ά, όν, 
Attic dialect μῶρος Hdn.Gr. 1.192: μῶρος as feminine, [Refs 5th c.BC+], but accusative μῶραν [Refs 3rd c.BC+]:—dull, sluggish, of the nerves, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; χειμῶνος ἀρχομένου μ. γίνονται οἱ ἐργάται [τῶν σφηκῶν] [Refs 4th c.BC+]
__2 of persons, dull, stupid, μωροῦ φωτὸς ἅδε βουλά [NT+5th c.BC+]: comparative in proverbial, μωρότερος Μορύχου [Refs 5th c.BC+]: superlative, [Refs 5th c.BC+] 
__2.b in a religious sense, λαὸς μ. καὶ ἀκάρδιος [LXX]
__3 of things, δέδοικα μῶρον πυραύστου μόρον [Refs 5th c.BC+]; τὸ μ. folly, [NT+5th c.BC+]; μῶρα φρονεῖν, φωνεῖν, δρᾶν, [LXX+5th c.BC+] 
__3.b μ. ἀνάγκη blind necessity, [Refs 4th c.BC+]
__4 of taste, insipid, flat, Comedy texts Adesp.[Refs 1st c.AD+]
__II adverb -ρως [Refs 5th c.BC+]. (Cf. Sanskrit mūrás 'idiot'.)

terms.lsj_entry:
μωρ-ία, 
Ionic dialect -ιη, ἡ, (μῶρος) folly, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; μωρίην ἐπιφέρειν τισί to impute folly to them, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; μωρίαν ὀφλισκάνειν to be charged with it, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; μωρίᾳ φιλονικεῖν foolishly, [Refs]; τῆς μ. what folly! [Refs 5th c.BC+]; of illicit love, [Refs 5th c.BC+]

terms.medium_def:
(act.) to make foolish, show one foolish; (pass.) to become a fool, be made a fool; (passive) to become saltless, tasteless, inert insipid 
to be foolish, to play the fool;, in NT transitive to make foolish, convict of folly, 1Cor. 1:20; passive to be convicted of folly, to incur the character of folly, Rom. 1:22; to be rendered insipid, lose savour, be tasteless Mt. 5:13; Lk. 14:34*
terms.lsj_entry:
μωρ-αίνω, 
future - ᾰνῶ [Refs 5th c.BC+]: aorist ἐμώρᾱνα [Refs 4th c.BC+] —passive, see below [Refs]: (μῶρος):—to be silly, foolish, drivel, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; play the fool, [Refs 4th c.BC+] make a mad attempt, [Refs 4th c.BC+]; οὐδεὶς.. ταῦτα μωραίνει indulges in these follies, [Refs 5th c.BC+]: euphemistic of illicit love, γυναῖκα μωραίνουσαν [Refs]
__II causal, make foolish, convict of folly, ἡ βουλὴ αὐτῶν μωρανθήσεται [LXX]; ἐμωράνθην σφόδρα[LXX+NT]:—passive, to become foolish, be stupefied, [αἶγες] ἑστᾶσιν ὥσπερ μεμωραμμέναι [Refs 4th c.BC+]; to become insipid, ἐὰν τὸ ἅλας μωρανθῇ [NT]
terms.short_def_mounce: (act.) to make foolish, show one foolish; (pass.) to become a fool, be made a fool; (passive) to become saltless, tasteless, inert insipid
